description_long: This object is a rectangular, box-shaped CD player with a flat top surface and minimal external detailing. The front face features two rectangular indents, likely representing the disc tray and a control panel or display area, while the sides and back are mostly plain with a few subtle panel lines. On the top surface, there is a raised section near one edge, possibly indicating the location of internal components or a ventilation area. The overall design is utilitarian, with sharp edges and a uniform, untextured finish. The object appears to be made primarily of plastic, with possible internal metal components for structural support and electronics. Its size and form suggest it is intended for tabletop or shelf placement in an urban environment.
description: A rectangular, flat-topped CD player with minimal external features and a raised section on the top. The front face has two rectangular indents, likely for the disc tray and controls.
description_view_0: The front view shows two rectangular indents, likely representing the disc tray and a control panel or display.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals a mostly plain surface with a small rectangular detail and the raised section on the top corner.
description_view_2: The back view is largely featureless except for the raised section extending from the top left, possibly for ventilation or ports.
description_view_3: The right side view is plain with subtle panel lines and the raised section visible on the top edge.
